name: DISTRAINT: Deluxe Edition
short_description: DISTRAINT is a 2D psychological horror adventure game for PC. In order to secure a partnership in a famous company, Price seizes the property of an elderly woman. In that very moment, he finds out the price of his humanity.
detailed_description:
DISTRAINT is a 2D psychological horror adventure game for PC.

You step into the shoes of an ambitious young man named Price.

In order to secure a partnership in a famous company, Price seizes the property of an elderly woman.
In that very moment, he finds out the price of his humanity.

This is his story and the tale of his regrets...

"We have yet to see you DANCE, son!"


_______________________________________________________



DISTRAINT is a horror-novel style of game. It is dark and grim but also has its share of dark humor.

The story progresses quickly which allows for several settings and scenarios.
Your journey will last around two hours!

The gameplay is simple but effective: You move left and right and solve puzzles to progress through the atmospheric story.

  • Side scrolling 2D with unique, hand drawn graphics
  • Atmospheric music and sound design
  • Minimalist interface so your focus never wavers from the experience
  • Delve into a unique story full of intriguing twists
  • One man effort - No asset flips or cheap tricks, just hard work!

"Snow... no wonder these folks are so absent."


_______________________________________________________



DISTRAINT has been doing quite well thanks to the awesome community!
Deluxe Edition is my thanks to you. Take a look at what's new:

  • Dynamic coloring - Goodbye gray, hello color!
  • No more lantern - Increased environmental lighting
  • Enhanced animation, graphics, and lighting effects
  • Refined audio for better atmosphere
  • Improved user interface

"These people are treated like cattle..."


_______________________________________________________

supported_languages: English, Italian, German, French, Spanish - Spain, Russian, Portuguese - Brazil, Simplified Chinese, Polish, Turkish, Korean, Japanese

reviews.review_text:
DISTRAINT had been sitting in my Steam library for around eight years. I remember playing it once back then and even recording a video for YouTube, but I never gave myself the chance to actually finish it. Recently I saw it again and thought: "why not now?" and it turned out to be a surprise. It is a short experience, but the story it tells carries a deeper message than you might expect, and its dark tone gives the game a very distinctive atmosphere.

That said, the gameplay itself is fairly simple and not very complex, and once you finish the story there is not much else to do. It is the kind of game that will entertain you for a couple of hours and leave you with something meaningful to think about, even if it is not necessarily an experience you will revisit often. Still, its emotional storytelling and clear message make it a memorable journey worth playing.

"Das Mitleid ist der Gegensatz der tonischen Empfindungen, welche die Energie des Lebensgefühls erhöhen: es wirkt depressiv. Man verliert Kraft, wenn man mitleidet."
This game has a very simple message. So simple in fact, that I feel like a child to whom the values of Christian morality are being explained in a rather patronizing tone.
Story premise is nonsensical and infantile as well. Why would McDade, Bruton & Moore make some errand boy their partner, even though all he did as far as player knows: just ran some basic errands? All I had left to do is guess that Price is actually a talented lawyer and that actual reasons for promotion were just left off-screen, and what we saw in game was merely a test of his ruthlessness. Also, how did he, as a partner, of an assumingly successful law firm stayed broke after few years of working there. Did they only promoted him, because he was working hard in the information sphere, but still too stupid to realize he should get a raise along with his new position too? Did Price just gave all of his money to charity, [spoiler]once he grew hateful towards himself[/spoiler]? There are many plot holes (huge ones to my concern) which just make story unbelievable.
The message is not only simple, but also worthless. Why should I sympathize with people who are evicted, because they have broken the law? Developer is obviously trying to squeeze out pity from the player, when it is said that Mrs. Goodwin fell and could not get up, but in a special institution she has more chances of living longer than alone, even at her home. Why doesn’t she have any relatives to look after her? It is the main reason for having children. That when you are old and useless - they would take care of you, out of a sense of duty.
Style of the game is OK, though.

80/100

I played this first time in 2016 when it was free on phone, so it's kind of nostalgic, even if the ambience is so gloomy. The story is not groundbreaking, but it is still a curious approach to the topics the game reflects.

I don't have too much to say about this one, it didn't grip me at all and it's scary moments were really just loud noises combined with scary images here and there, the atmosphere of the game doesn't really get to you as all you're doing is moving left and right solving a very easy puzzle and onto the next part, it's a very simple game that can be beat in one sitting, but honestly i couldn't recommend this to people, there are better games out there to be played.
you might finish it in one sitting but it will likely stay on your mind for days
